rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography
Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa)
rules_evaluated.evidence: 5:1–3 | 7–24:entire thesis lands in peer-warm Godin + counterintuitive Sutherland register

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream
rules_evaluated.evidence: 3:opening 'You just did something a chat can't do' is sharp; carve-out in 4–5 names the three specific things that aren't the point

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy, NOT student-facing curriculum body
rules_evaluated.evidence: lecture stays grounded in what the student-just-did (Phase 3) and mechanism (persistence + automation + loop), no defensive positioning against vendors

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'compounding' is the lecture's title and subject; appears first at 5, earned immediately by narrative of what the student just did; no premature planting of LLM-internals or platform vocabulary
entire lecture names capacities (building systems, steering via memory, compounding knowledge) not gaps

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'compounding' earned by deed in 5; 'memory' earned as 'files on disk' in 11; 'automation' earned as 'instructions in a file the agent reads' in 12; 'context' earned via narrative in 20

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing stays out of body
rules_evaluated.evidence: body prose is all second-person student-facing; maintainer content below divider at line 56; no delivery mechanics in body
no em-dashes anywhere in body or prompts

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: 46:lead-in names the prompt action in 1 sentence (ask Claude to spot, tell me, ask one question per specific page) before the **Prompt** label

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block
rules_evaluated.evidence: 46–50:proper shape; **Prompt** label with (Claude Code) destination; no copy → prefix; no (end of prompt) closer
prompt block contains no [BRACKETS] or placeholder syntax; open-hook pattern not needed (Claude has full scrollback context)

lecture lands insight-and-optimism mood: unease at scale (how does compounding work?) resolved by mechanism (persistence + automation + loop) and next-module setup (M2 adds shelf life). Mood drives into M2 opening on memory as shared context.

lecture introduces 'compounding' as the core M2 discipline and frames it via the student's just-lived Phase 3 (building + refreshing memory + tighter claims). No forward-run of M3/M4/M5 mechanics. Aligns with Module 2's Big Idea and sequence.
